definition: Cross-asset markets exhibit a growth consensus without rate consensus: equities price 5-6% nominal GDP (S&P ~21x fwd P/E, ~10% EPS growth), rates price 3-4% (forwards embed 75-100bp of easing), with credit and FX in between. This divergence must resolve, with rates markets having been the better leading indicator ~60-65% of the time historically.
evidence: Equity-implied NGDP of 5-6% vs rates-implied 3-4% — quantifiable divergence
pair_0 called this the single most actionable observation
Historical base rates favor rates markets ~60-65% of the time as leading indicator
Post-COVID rates market track record is poor, reducing historical base rate reliability
open_questions: Could the divergence persist in a Goldilocks muddling-through scenario?
Is the 60-65% historical base rate for rates accuracy sourced and robust?
Which asset class will prove correct in this cycle?
